behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (manifest validation must gate module import), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no subprocess/network/external API is required; local temp plugin directory plus loader wiring is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Unit is too low because the behavior explicitly requires proving plugin code is never executed, which needs validator+loader+import path together; System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text has no real OS primitive beyond normal local file reads, no AI call, and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugins directory with one plugin whose manifest omits a required field and whose entry module has an observable side effect (e.g., appends to an in-memory log/file). Run the real loader against that directory; assert loader reports manifest rejection and side effect never occurs.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bug appears in interaction of manifest parser, dependency resolver, and lifecycle invoker. Unit can test topo-sort alone but not actual hook call ordering through loader pipeline. REAL-INFRA: no real HTTP/subprocess/OS-specific behavior needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System/Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (pure local plugin loading and hook invocation).
behaviors.test_description: Set up temp directory with plugins A and B where A depends on B; each onLoad pushes its name into a shared call log. Execute loader and assert log order is ["B","A"].

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure boundary is resolver output affecting executor behavior (cycle detection must prevent any loads). Unit cycle-algorithm checks miss integration bug where loader still executes hooks despite cycle flag. REAL-INFRA: local files only; no subprocess/network required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior only concerns local dependency graph handling.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B with mutual dependencies and onLoad side-effect logs. Run loader; assert cycle error/report is produced and neither plugin’s onLoad side effect is observed.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires sequencing across module import/execute and lifecycle hook dispatch. Unit tests of hook runner alone cannot catch import-before-hook contract violations. REAL-INFRA: no subprocess/network/external API; standard in-process module loading with local files is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior does not require OS-level eventing or subprocess semantics; Agentic/Workflow rejected due to no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin entry module that sets a global/shared marker during module top-level execution; onLoad asserts marker is already set and records success/failure. Run loader and assert onLoad runs only after marker is set.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state-propagation across dependency graph and error handling (failed plugin, dependent skip, independent continue). Unit tests of try/catch logic alone miss resolver+scheduler interactions. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed; (b) no AI/ML output dependency; (c) no multi-agent chain; (d) no LLM-involved stage handoff. Rejection gate: higher levels disqualified by behavior text’s purely local plugin lifecycle semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: F (throws in onLoad), D (depends on F), and I (independent). Run loader; assert F failure is reported, D is not initialized (or marked blocked by dependency failure), and I’s onLoad still runs.
